level_signals: 5+ years of experience as a TPM, PM, or Software Engineer
Base salary range $211,200–$264,000 consistent with Senior IC at Scale AI

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience as a Technical Program Manager, Product Manager, or Software Engineer, with a proven track record of having built and shipped technical products or platforms from scratch (e.g., internal cloud infrastructure, developer APIs, distributed systems, or ML platforms).

Platform Domain Expertise: 3+ years of dedicated experience managing programs focused directly on core engineering infrastructure, cloud-native ecosystems (AWS/GCP), container orchestration (Kubernetes), or distributed systems.

AI/ML Infrastructure Literacy: Foundational understanding of the infrastructure required for the Generative AI lifecycle, including high-throughput data pipelines, GPU/CPU cluster utilization, or model training/evaluation setups.

Masterful Communication: Proven track record of presenting to and influencing executive-level stakeholders, with the ability to translate complex technical/architectural challenges into clear business impacts.
Execution Excellence: Advanced proficiency with iterative development methodologies and modern project management tooling (Linear, Jira, etc.) applied to foundational infrastructure environments.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Engineering Roots: Strong software engineering fundamentals, with prior professional experience as a Software Engineer, DevOps Engineer, or Data Developer before transitioning into program management.
Platform Adoption Track Record: Proven success driving the internal adoption of technical platforms, SDKs, or APIs across disparate, fast-moving product lines.
Data-Centric AI Familiarity: Direct experience working with large-scale data quality pipelines, distributed vector databases, or specialized AI inference engines (e.g., Triton, Ray).

scope.surface: Scale Generative AI Platform (SGP) — orchestration layers, model serving, APIs, core infrastructure
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise AI platform and data services
core_responsibilities: Lead strategic planning and high-velocity execution for SGP core capabilities (orchestration layers, model serving, APIs)
Drive execution and manage complex technical dependencies across systems engineering, Core ML, Research, and Product teams
Translate complex infrastructure metrics into actionable roadmaps
Proactively identify, track, and mitigate technical risks unique to massive-scale GenAI infrastructure
Establish lightweight agile processes that empower engineers to ship fast without breaking core systems
Define and enforce clear SLOs and performance benchmarks
Track and report on SGP adoption metrics, system reliability, delivery forecasts, and engineering bottlenecks directly to executive leadership

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (RSUs/options subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($211,200–$264,000 base) with equity on top
Clear product surface with named platform (SGP) and specific technical domains
Role has direct executive visibility and strategic impact
Company has named enterprise and government customers indicating stability and scale
Inclusive and equal opportunity employer statements present
red_flags: Location appears onsite-only in SF, NY, or Seattle with no mention of remote eligibility — eliminates fully remote candidates
90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role is an unusual policy worth noting
High buzzword density ('hyper-growth', 'connective tissue', 'masterful communication') may obscure actual role clarity
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Very broad year-range for seniority band could compress compensation offer despite stated range

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years requirement, and executive-facing responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as KPIs)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from 'Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located' and pay transparency statement listing only SF, NY, Seattle)
ambiguities: Location type: The JD provides salary ranges only for SF, NY, and Seattle, and says to 'reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located,' which implies onsite; however, the JD does not explicitly say 'no remote,' creating mild ambiguity. Treated as onsite here.
Visa sponsorship: Not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Classified as 'unclear'.
The compensation note says the range 'may be inclusive of several career levels at Scale,' making it hard to pinpoint exact seniority tier from comp alone.
